question: Will Marine Le Pen poll at or above 34% in the first round of the most recent 2027 French presidential voting-intention poll listed on Wikipedia before July 25, 2026?

factors.description: All post-candidacy polls testing Le Pen by name cluster at 34-37% with highest-scenario figures 35.5-37%, all above the 34% threshold.
Resolution uses the highest Le Pen figure among scenarios; every recent poll's high end is >=34%, so even lower-scenario polls resolve YES.
Candidacy announcement (7 July) triggered upward momentum, lifting Le Pen from ~31-33% (May) to 34-37% (July).
Risk: a new low-RN-house-effect pollster could field the final pre-July-25 poll with a max scenario <=33.9%, flipping to NO.

summary: The question resolves on the single most recent qualifying poll (Le Pen tested by name as RN candidate) listed on Wikipedia as of 24 July 2026, using her HIGHEST reported first-round figure. Since her 7 July candidacy announcement, every poll testing her by name shows 34-37% (Verian ~37%, Elabe 34-35.5%, OpinionWay 35%, Harris 34-36%, Ifop 36%), with all high-end scenarios comfortably above 34%. The 'highest figure' rule reinforces YES. The main NO path is a single new low-house-effect pollster fielding the final pre-July-25 poll with a max scenario below 34% — plausible given house-effect variance and April's 32-33% Harris reading, but contrary to the strong current clustering and post-candidacy momentum. Summer polling lull may mean the most recent poll is an already-listed July poll (all YES). Estimate ~0.89.
reasoning:
My forecast is **89% YES**.

**Reasoning:** This question hinges on which single poll is "most recent" on Wikipedia as of July 24, 2026, and whether Marine Le Pen's highest reported first-round figure is ≥34%. Since her July 7 candidacy announcement (after the appeals court shortened her ineligibility), a wave of polls testing her by name has appeared, all clustering at 34-37%: Verian ~37%, Elabe 34-35.5%, OpinionWay 35%, Harris 34-36%, Ifop 36%. Every one of these has a highest-scenario figure at or above 34%, and the resolution rule uses the *highest* figure when a poll reports multiple scenarios — further favoring YES.

The main path to NO is a single new pollster with a low RN house effect fielding the final poll before July 25 and reporting Le Pen's max scenario below 34% (recent enough given April's 32-33% Harris reading). But this runs against the strong current clustering and post-candidacy momentum, and the summer polling lull increases the chance the "most recent" poll is simply one of the already-listed July polls — all of which resolve YES.
